description: Originally founded as an enforcement wing and debt collection service for Pablo Escobar's Medellín Cartel, Oficina de Envigado evolved into a major drug cartel and criminal organization based in Medellín and Envigado, Colombia. It controls extortion, gambling, and money laundering in the Valle de Aburrá and acts as a mediator and debt collector in drug trafficking disputes. By 2019, its activities shifted from direct drug trafficking to providing services to lower-level traffickers and mafia groups. It maintains connections with Colombian paramilitaries and guerrillas.

country_links.evidence_quote: La Oficina also has links with local authority figures and police officers... and it has alliances with Los Rastrojos and Los Zetas in Mexico.
country_links.context: The organization has alliances with Los Zetas in Mexico.

country_links.evidence_quote: La Oficina eventually evolved into a sizable narco-trafficking operation spanning from Medellín to the northern coast of Colombia, including the Panamanian border area...
country_links.context: The organization's narco-trafficking operation spans into the Panamanian border area.

country_links.evidence_quote: On July 20, 2014, Hernán Alonso Villa... was stopped en route to one of his safe houses in the south-eastern province of Alicante carrying €40,000... which... came from drug running and assassinations
country_links.context: A leader of the organization's military wing was stopped en route to a safe house in Spain carrying cash from drug running and assassinations.
